name: Fideus with Clams and Garlic Mayonnaise
recipeIngredient: 1 leek (white and pale green parts only)
1 1/2 pounds fish bones from white fish
recipeInstructions:
1. Whisk together mayonnaise, garlic, and buttermilk in a small bowl until smooth. Stir in chives. Season with salt and pepper. Cover and refrigerate for 1 hour to allow flavors to blend.
2. Heat 1 tablespoon olive oil in a heavy large Dutch oven over medium heat. Add onion and leek and sauté until tender but not brown, about 10 minutes.
3. Add wine and boil until almost all liquid evaporates, about 2 minutes. Add fish bones, water, parsley, peppercorns, and bay leaf. Simmer until liquid reduces to 6 cups, about 1 hour. Strain; discard solids.
4. Heat remaining 2 tablespoons olive oil in a heavy large Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Break pasta into 2-inch lengths and add to the pot. Sauté until pasta is golden brown, about 5 minutes.
5. Add stock. Cook until pasta is almost tender but mixture is still soupy, about 3 minutes. Add clams, pushing them into the broth. Cover and cook until clams open, about 8 minutes.
6. Discard any clams that do not open. Season with salt and pepper. Serve hot.
Yield: 4 servings
